note_html: Subjective: HPI: The patient, a 35-year-old male, presented with a 2-hour history of low back pain and tingling sensation in the right foot, which started after raking leaves in the yard. He reports that the pain worsened when standing up and improved with a hot shower and sitting. He denies any previous similar episodes lasting more than a day. He reports trying ice and 2 Advil without relief. His partner is present and reports similar pain when the patient stands up. Past medical history: The patient has a history of similar low back pain 3 years ago that resolved on its own. He normally does not engage in physical activity, and his partner also reports not being physically active. Review of systems: The patient reports tingling sensation in the right leg. Current medications: None mentioned. Objective: Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED] Physical exam: The patient has some pain with flexion, palpation at L5, and dorsiflexion is normal. Pulses are equal in all extremities. Test Results: X-ray of the low back is normal. Assessment / Problem List: Assessment: The patient has a probable lumbar strain with possible sprain, likely secondary to heavy lifting and bending while raking leaves. Problem list: Lumbar strain; suspected sprain; [NOT MENTIONED] Plan: I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg orally once daily for pain management. I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretching and exercises to improve flexibility and strength. The patient and his partner will follow up in 2 weeks to re-evaluate the condition, and if not improving, we will consider further imaging, such as an MRI. Follow-up: The patient will follow up in 2 weeks to re-evaluate the condition and consider further imaging if necessary.
